user_query:
As the spokesperson for the Canadian Snowboard Association, I need an automated, repeatable way to build a small media briefing dataset from official, public sources. Please implement a runnable script that uses live web search to find relevant pages, downloads them, and extracts structured fields for our briefing. Inputs and outputs must be exactly as below.

Inputs (provided in this workspace):
- input/queries.json: A JSON file containing search queries, an ID for each query, allowed domain filters, and a max_results cap.
- input/keywords.json: A JSON file containing English and French keywords to detect in downloaded pages.

Required behavior:
1) For each item in input/queries.json, perform a web search using the specified "query" string. Do not hardcode target URLs; you must discover pages via search. Filter the results to those whose host matches one of the "allowed_domains" for that query_id. Process up to max_results per query_id.
2) For each filtered URL, download the page HTML and save a local copy under output/pages/<query_id>/ as sequentially numbered files (e.g., 1.html, 2.html, ...). Create the subdirectory if it does not exist. Avoid duplicates across the whole run.
3) From each saved HTML file, extract these fields into a single CSV at output/pages_metadata.csv with columns in this exact order: query_id, url, source_domain, saved_path, http_status, title, h1, published_date_iso, matched_keywords, content_length. Field definitions:
   - query_id: The query_id from input/queries.json.
   - url: The final URL fetched.
   - source_domain: The hostname of the URL.
   - saved_path: The relative path where the HTML was saved (e.g., output/pages/csb_official_news/3.html).
   - http_status: The HTTP status code returned when downloading (200, 404, etc.).
   - title: The <title> text.
   - h1: The text of the first <h1> if present, otherwise blank.
   - published_date_iso: Best-effort extraction of a publication date in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD or full timestamp) from common meta tags (e.g., article:published_time, og:published_time, meta[name="date"], time tag) or leave blank if not found.
   - matched_keywords: Semicolon-separated list of any keywords from input/keywords.json (English or French) found in the visible text content of the page; if none, leave blank.
   - content_length: Size in bytes of the saved HTML file.
4) Also write a JSON manifest at output/pages_manifest.json containing an array of objects with: {"query_id", "url", "saved_path", "http_status"}. Include one entry per saved page.

Constraints and notes:
- Use only publicly accessible web search (no paid API keys). The script must actually issue search engine queries at runtime and discover URLs from the results.
- Only include pages whose host matches the allowed_domains for the relevant query_id.
- Do not hardcode any target URLs.
- If a page cannot be downloaded or parsed, record the attempted URL with the http_status (or an error string) and continue with other results.
- The script should run with no arguments, reading the two input files and writing all outputs to the output/ folder as specified.

Deliverables to create in this workspace:
- A runnable script at tools/build_media_briefing.py that implements the above logic.
- On execution (e.g., python tools/build_media_briefing.py), the script must produce:
  - Saved HTML pages under output/pages/<query_id>/
  - output/pages_metadata.csv with the exact columns and order described
  - output/pages_manifest.json

Do not include any private credentials or paid services. Please ensure the three internet-related steps are performed: live web search, remote page downloads, and structured extraction from the downloaded HTML.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
